# Day 1: Data Import
#Load these libraries
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
#Import dataset, keep the csv in the data folder
nhis_data <- read.csv("data/NHIS _Data_2021.csv")
#Initial Exploration no cleaning
cat("Data Structure:\n")
## Data Structure:
str(nhis_data)
## 'data.frame':    29482 obs. of  18 variables:
##  $ DEMENEV_A   : int  2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 ...
##  $ COPDEV_A    : int  2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 ...
##  $ HYPEV_A     : int  1 1 2 1 2 1 2 2 2 2 ...
##  $ DEPEV_A     : int  2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 1 ...
##  $ CANEV_A     : int  2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 ...
##  $ DIBEV_A     : int  2 1 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 2 ...
##  $ AGEP_A      : int  50 53 56 57 25 55 45 41 26 71 ...
##  $ SEX_A       : int  1 1 1 2 1 1 1 1 2 2 ...
##  $ HISPALLP_A  : int  2 3 2 2 3 3 2 3 3 2 ...
##  $ MARSTAT_A   : int  5 6 5 7 9 9 1 1 7 1 ...
##  $ EDUCP_A     : int  1 7 8 5 4 5 9 5 4 9 ...
##  $ PHSTAT_A    : int  2 2 2 4 3 3 1 1 2 1 ...
##  $ LSATIS4R_A  : int  2 1 3 2 8 8 1 1 1 1 ...
##  $ SMKCIGST_A  : int  3 4 3 3 9 9 4 4 4 4 ...
##  $ RATCAT_A    : int  7 12 14 11 6 6 14 14 7 14 ...
##  $ BMICAT_A    : int  3 3 3 4 4 3 9 3 4 2 ...
##  $ WEIGHTLBTC_A: int  199 205 160 190 250 200 997 206 996 127 ...
##  $ HEIGHTTC_A  : int  69 75 67 63 72 69 67 72 96 63 ...
cat("\nSumarry Statistics:\n")
## 
## Sumarry Statistics:
summary(nhis_data)
##    DEMENEV_A        COPDEV_A        HYPEV_A         DEPEV_A     
##  Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000  
##  1st Qu.:2.000   1st Qu.:2.000   1st Qu.:1.000   1st Qu.:2.000  
##  Median :2.000   Median :2.000   Median :2.000   Median :2.000  
##  Mean   :1.993   Mean   :1.951   Mean   :1.648   Mean   :1.829  
##  3rd Qu.:2.000   3rd Qu.:2.000   3rd Qu.:2.000   3rd Qu.:2.000  
##  Max.   :9.000   Max.   :9.000   Max.   :9.000   Max.   :9.000  
##     CANEV_A         DIBEV_A        AGEP_A          SEX_A         HISPALLP_A   
##  Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.0   Min.   :18.00   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000  
##  1st Qu.:2.000   1st Qu.:2.0   1st Qu.:37.00   1st Qu.:1.000   1st Qu.:2.000  
##  Median :2.000   Median :2.0   Median :53.00   Median :2.000   Median :2.000  
##  Mean   :1.882   Mean   :1.9   Mean   :52.63   Mean   :1.547   Mean   :2.203  
##  3rd Qu.:2.000   3rd Qu.:2.0   3rd Qu.:68.00   3rd Qu.:2.000   3rd Qu.:2.000  
##  Max.   :9.000   Max.   :9.0   Max.   :99.00   Max.   :9.000   Max.   :7.000  
##    MARSTAT_A        EDUCP_A          PHSTAT_A      LSATIS4R_A   
##  Min.   :1.000   Min.   : 1.000   Min.   :1.00   Min.   :1.000  
##  1st Qu.:1.000   1st Qu.: 4.000   1st Qu.:2.00   1st Qu.:1.000  
##  Median :4.000   Median : 6.000   Median :2.00   Median :2.000  
##  Mean   :3.858   Mean   : 6.447   Mean   :2.39   Mean   :1.748  
##  3rd Qu.:7.000   3rd Qu.: 8.000   3rd Qu.:3.00   3rd Qu.:2.000  
##  Max.   :9.000   Max.   :99.000   Max.   :9.00   Max.   :9.000  
##    SMKCIGST_A       RATCAT_A         BMICAT_A      WEIGHTLBTC_A  
##  Min.   :1.000   Min.   : 1.000   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :100.0  
##  1st Qu.:3.000   1st Qu.: 7.000   1st Qu.:2.000   1st Qu.:150.0  
##  Median :4.000   Median :11.000   Median :3.000   Median :180.0  
##  Mean   :3.579   Mean   : 9.848   Mean   :3.121   Mean   :248.8  
##  3rd Qu.:4.000   3rd Qu.:14.000   3rd Qu.:4.000   3rd Qu.:215.0  
##  Max.   :9.000   Max.   :14.000   Max.   :9.000   Max.   :999.0  
##    HEIGHTTC_A   
##  Min.   :59.00  
##  1st Qu.:64.00  
##  Median :67.00  
##  Mean   :68.72  
##  3rd Qu.:70.00  
##  Max.   :99.00
cat("\nFirst 6 Rows:\n")
## 
## First 6 Rows:
head(nhis_data)
##   DEMENEV_A COPDEV_A HYPEV_A DEPEV_A CANEV_A DIBEV_A AGEP_A SEX_A HISPALLP_A
## 1         2        2       1       2       2       2     50     1          2
## 2         2        2       1       2       2       1     53     1          3
## 3         2        2       2       2       2       2     56     1          2
## 4         2        2       1       2       2       2     57     2          2
## 5         2        2       2       2       2       2     25     1          3
## 6         2        2       1       2       2       2     55     1          3
##   MARSTAT_A EDUCP_A PHSTAT_A LSATIS4R_A SMKCIGST_A RATCAT_A BMICAT_A
## 1         5       1        2          2          3        7        3
## 2         6       7        2          1          4       12        3
## 3         5       8        2          3          3       14        3
## 4         7       5        4          2          3       11        4
## 5         9       4        3          8          9        6        4
## 6         9       5        3          8          9        6        3
##   WEIGHTLBTC_A HEIGHTTC_A
## 1          199         69
## 2          205         75
## 3          160         67
## 4          190         63
## 5          250         72
## 6          200         69
# Day 2: Data Cleaning
library(tidyverse)

#Need to pick a variable and check if anythings missing
nhis_clean <- nhis_data %>%
# Selecting only what I need
select(AGEP_A, WEIGHTLBTC_A, HEIGHTTC_A, SEX_A, HISPALLP_A, 
         EDUCP_A, PHSTAT_A, LSATIS4R_A) %>%
#Remove missing values, look at the codebook
filter(
    !AGEP_A %in% c(97, 98, 99),
    !WEIGHTLBTC_A %in% c(996, 997, 998, 999),
    !HEIGHTTC_A %in% c(96, 97, 98, 99),
    !SEX_A %in% c(7, 9),
    !EDUCP_A %in% c(97, 98, 99),
    !PHSTAT_A %in% c(7, 8, 9),
    !LSATIS4R_A %in% c(7, 8, 9)
  )
# got to use the recode command
nhis_clean <- nhis_clean %>%
  mutate(
#Recode EDUCP_A
    EDUCP_A_recoded = case_when(
      EDUCP_A %in% 0:4 ~ "1 - Less than High School",
      EDUCP_A == 4 ~ "2 - High School Graduate", # (Note: adjusted to fit instructions, usually 0-3 and 4)
      EDUCP_A %in% 5:7 ~ "3 - Some College",
      EDUCP_A %in% 8:10 ~ "4 - College Grad or higher",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
#Convert to factor
EDUCP_A_recoded = factor(EDUCP_A_recoded, 
                             levels = c("1 - Less than High School", 
                                        "2 - High School Graduate", 
                                        "3 - Some College", 
                                        "4 - College Grad or higher")),
#Format the other Qualitative stuff to make it easier to read
SEX_A = factor(SEX_A, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Male", "Female")),
    PHSTAT_A = factor(PHSTAT_A, levels = c(1,2,3,4,5), 
                      labels = c("Excellent", "Very Good", "Good", "Fair", "Poor")),
    LSATIS4R_A = factor(LSATIS4R_A, levels = c(1,2,3,4), 
                        labels = c("Very Satisfied", "Satisfied", "Dissatisfied", "Very Dissatisfied"))
  ) %>%
#Drop any rows that had NA
drop_na()

#This is to save it
write_csv(nhis_clean, "data/nhis_clean.csv")
print("Data cleaning complete. File saved to data/nhis_clean.csv")
## [1] "Data cleaning complete. File saved to data/nhis_clean.csv"
#Day 3: The Univariate Information
library(tidyverse)

nhis_clean <- read_csv("data/nhis_clean.csv") %>%
  mutate(across(c(SEX_A, EDUCP_A_recoded, PHSTAT_A, LSATIS4R_A), as.factor))
## Rows: 26037 Columns: 9
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (4): SEX_A, PHSTAT_A, LSATIS4R_A, EDUCP_A_recoded
## dbl (5): AGEP_A, WEIGHTLBTC_A, HEIGHTTC_A, HISPALLP_A, EDUCP_A
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
# Quantitative Var
quant_vars <-c("AGEP_A", "WEIGHTLBTC_A", "HEIGHTTC_A")

#Summary I need to see
summary(nhis_clean[quant_vars])
##      AGEP_A       WEIGHTLBTC_A     HEIGHTTC_A  
##  Min.   :18.00   Min.   :100.0   Min.   :59.0  
##  1st Qu.:37.00   1st Qu.:147.0   1st Qu.:64.0  
##  Median :54.00   Median :173.0   Median :66.0  
##  Mean   :52.57   Mean   :176.8   Mean   :66.7  
##  3rd Qu.:67.00   3rd Qu.:200.0   3rd Qu.:70.0  
##  Max.   :85.00   Max.   :299.0   Max.   :76.0
#Standard Deviation
sapply(nhis_clean[quant_vars], sd)
##       AGEP_A WEIGHTLBTC_A   HEIGHTTC_A 
##    18.334836    39.595379     3.898793
# Somebody fill the rest on line 90 onward
# Histograms
print(
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = AGEP_A)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  labs(title = "Age Distribution", x = "Age", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
)
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.

print(
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = WEIGHTLBTC_A)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  labs(title = "Weight Distribution", x = "Weight", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
)
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.

print(
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = HEIGHTTC_A)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  labs(title = "Height Distribution", x = "Height", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
)
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.

# Boxplots
print(
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(y = AGEP_A)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Age Boxplot", y = "Age") +
  theme_minimal()
)

print(
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(y = WEIGHTLBTC_A)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Weight Boxplot", y = "Weight") +
  theme_minimal()
)

print(
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(y = HEIGHTTC_A)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Height Boxplot", y = "Height") +
  theme_minimal()
)

# Bar Plots
print(
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = SEX_A)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "Sex Distribution", x = "Sex", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
)

print(
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = EDUCP_A_recoded)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "Education Distribution", x = "Education", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
)

#Correlation coefficient
correlation_value <- cor(nhis_clean$HEIGHTTC_A, nhis_clean$WEIGHTLBTC_A, use = "complete.obs")
cat("Correlation Between Height vs Weight", correlation_value, "\n")
## Correlation Between Height vs Weight 0.5023037
#Scatter Plots
#Base R scatter plot
plot(nhis_clean$HEIGHTTC_A, nhis_clean$WEIGHTLBTC_A,
     main = "Height vs Weight",
     xlab = "Height in Inches",
     ylab = "Weight in Pounds",
     col = "blue")

#ggplot2 scatter plot
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = HEIGHTTC_A, y = WEIGHTLBTC_A)) +
  geom_point(color = "pink") +
  labs(title = "Scatterplot of Height vs Weight", x = "Height in Inches", y = "Weight in Pounds") +
  theme_minimal()

#Day 4: Multivariate Analysis & Report Drafting
#load library that is going to be used
library(psych)
## 
## Attaching package: 'psych'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     %+%, alpha
#enhanced scatter plot from Day 3
ggplot(nhis_clean, aes(x = HEIGHTTC_A , y = WEIGHTLBTC_A, color = SEX_A)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~ EDUCP_A) +
  labs(
    title = "Height vs Weight by Sex and Educational Level",
    x = "Height in Inches",
    y = "Weight in Pounds",
    color = "Sex"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

correlation_variables <- nhis_clean %>%
  select(AGEP_A, WEIGHTLBTC_A, HEIGHTTC_A)
pairs.panels(correlation_variables,
             method = "pearson",
             hist.col = "lightblue",
             density = TRUE,
             ellipses = TRUE,
             main = "Correlation Matrix: Age, Weight, and Height")
